description:
GOAL: Wire f64 values through the JIT, end-to-end. Today `Const::Float`, float arithmetic, and float bit fields all panic in ir_codegen — boxed floats unblock all three.

## Why this ticket exists

The FzValue scheme uses low-bit tags 0..3 for ptr/int/atom/special, leaving 1xx "reserved (future: boxed float, etc.)" (see src/fz_value.rs). The JIT was authored without picking one of those tags, so any float in a JIT'd program crashes:

- `Const::Float(_)` → CodegenError "Float/Str codegen lands in .11.10+"
- Float bit fields (`<<x::float-32>>`) panic in fz_bs_write_field / fz_bs_read_field (deferred from fz-ul4.11.12, 2026-05-10)
- BinOp on floats has no path at all

## Decisions captured

- v1 representation: **heap-allocated** boxed f64. New `HeapKind::Float = 8` (extend the enum in src/fz_value.rs). Layout: `HeapHeader (16) + f64 (8) + pad (8)` = 32 bytes. Uses Tag::Ptr (low bits 000) like every other heap value. Reserved 1xx tags stay free for future inline NaN-boxing if/when profiling justifies the complexity. Heap allocation is the pessimistic case; correct first, fast second.
- `HeapKind::Float` is a leaf type for the GC tracer: raw payload, no FzValue children to follow. Add to the `Bitstring | VecI64 | ...` branch in src/heap.rs::trace.
- Comparisons of two boxed floats compare by value, NOT by ptr identity (`Eq` on FzValues currently uses `icmp eq, av, bvv` which would fail for two equal-valued but distinct-allocated floats). The IR-level `BinOp::Eq` path needs to detect float operands and dispatch to `fz_float_eq` runtime fn (or specialized cranelift `fcmp`). Same for Lt/Le/Gt/Ge.
- No auto-promotion of Int + Float → Float. The IR doesn't carry type info per-value at runtime, so codegen for `BinOp::Add(a, b)` continues to assume int-int (current behavior). A separate ticket can add unboxed-float-fast-path arithmetic when the typer feeds enough info to ir_codegen; v1 routes mixed-type arithmetic through a runtime helper that dispatches on tag.

## Deliverables

src/fz_value.rs:
- Add `HeapKind::Float = 8` and update `HeapKind::from_u16`.
- (Optional) Helper `alloc_float(f64) -> *mut HeapHeader` for tests / interp side.

src/heap.rs:
- `Heap::alloc_float(f: f64) -> *mut HeapHeader` (raw alloc, header init, payload write).
- `HeapKind::Float` arm in `trace()` (alongside other raw-payload kinds).

src/ir_codegen.rs:
- Runtime fns: `fz_alloc_float(bits: u64) -> u64` (returns ptr-tagged FzValue), `fz_float_add / sub / mul / div / mod` taking two FzValue ptr-bits, returning a fresh boxed float, and float comparison helpers (`fz_float_eq / lt / le / gt / ge`) returning tagged TRUE/FALSE.
- `Const::Float(f)` lowering: emit a const-data alloc at compile time (or call `fz_alloc_float` lazily via a constant table).
- Tag-dispatch for `BinOp::Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod`: peek at low bits of operands; if both Tag::Int → existing fast path; if either is Tag::Ptr to HeapKind::Float → call `fz_float_*`. Cranelift can do this with a small if/else around the existing fast path.
- Same for ordered comparisons.
- `BinOp::Eq` / `Neq` already work for ptr identity but must now also handle "two distinct boxed floats with same value". Route through a runtime helper when at least one operand is a float ptr.
- Float bit fields in `fz_bs_write_field` / `fz_bs_read_field`: replace the panic paths with real implementations using `apply_endian_for_write` + bit casts.
- `render_fz_value` extends to `HeapKind::Float`: Rust's `{}` for f64 with explicit `.0` for whole numbers (`3.14` / `4.0`).

## Tests

- `fn main(), do: 3.14` halts with bits decodable as f64 = 3.14.
- `print(3.14)` captures `"3.14"`.
- `1.5 + 2.5 == 4.0` via JIT (BinOp arithmetic + comparison).
- `print(<<3.14::float-64>>)` constructs an 8-byte bitstring; `<<x::float-64>> = ...` round-trips back to 3.14.
- Allocate float, drop root, GC reclaims.
- Mixed-type comparison: `1 == 1.0` returns false (different reps; v1 doesn't promote — document explicitly).

## Out of scope

- Inline NaN-boxing / denormalized-payload tricks for performance.
- Auto-promotion of int + float → float (typer-driven, future ticket).
- f32 (everything is f64 for now; bit fields read/write 32-bit floats by bit-casting).
- Specialized hot-path unboxing in user-defined float-only fns (deferred to .11.15 frame inliner / typer integration).

## Cross-references

- Reinstates float bit fields deferred at .11.12 landing (2026-05-10); the relevant test bodies are already gated under `#![cfg(any())]` in src/{codegen,jit,aot}.rs and should be lifted into ir_codegen tests (or new tests in tests/) as part of this ticket.
- Should land before any ticket that wants to expose individual f64 elements from a Vec(F64) as scalars (currently no such ticket; .11.14 Vec(F64) handles raw payload only).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: none — the supporting heap machinery (HeapKind, allocation, tracer) is in place; this is incremental wiring atop fz-ul4.11.{2,12,18}.
- BLOCKS: none currently filed. Add a `bw dep add fz-ul4.11.20 blocks <id>` if a future ticket needs float scalars.
title: Boxed floats in the JIT
